steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that Newland compares his intellect favorably to that of other men in the audience — he must judge his own mind to be superior to theirs.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'distinctly the superior ... read more, thought more': Directly states Newland felt intellectually above the other men, which is exactly a favorable self-comparison. ✓
  • Choice B — about reality: Concerns Newland's perception of reality, not a comparison of intellects. ✗
  • Choice C — the men's influence on him: Admits the audience had some effect on Newland — the opposite of him feeling superior. ✗
  • Choice D — describes the audience without comparison: Characterizes the other men but makes no claim about Newland's mind relative to theirs. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
